body:
# proto.cr

[![Test](https://github.com/kojix2/proto/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/kojix2/proto/actions/workflows/test.yml)
[![Lines of Code](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftokei.kojix2.net%2Fbadge%2Fgithub%2Fkojix2%2Fproto%2Flines)](https://tokei.kojix2.net/github/kojix2/proto)

📦 [Protobuf](https://protobuf.dev/) runtime and protoc plugin for Crystal.

This library was generated using an AI agent.

## Installation

```yaml
dependencies:
  proto:
    github: kojix2/proto
```

## Build plugin

```sh
shards build
```

## Generate .pb.cr

```sh
protoc --plugin=protoc-gen-crystal=bin/protoc-gen-crystal \
  --crystal_out=. path/to/example.proto
```

Generated .pb.cr files include:

- message / enum definitions
- RPC metadata for each service
- extension metadata at file and message scope

Client/server stubs are not generated for services themselves. For gRPC stubs, use grpc.cr's protoc-gen-crystal-grpc.

## Runtime usage

```crystal
require "proto"
require "./example.pb.cr"

msg = Example::HelloRequest.new
msg.name = "Alice"

bytes = msg.encode
parsed = Example::HelloRequest.decode(bytes)
puts parsed.name
```

Generated message types expose two wire-level API layers:

- `decode` / `encode`: strict APIs that validate required fields across the full message tree
- `decode_partial` / `encode_partial`: explicit partial APIs for working with incomplete messages

Strict validation failures raise `Proto::RequiredFieldError`, which is a `Proto::ValidationError`.

```crystal
begin
  parsed = Example::Settings.decode(bytes)
rescue ex : Proto::RequiredFieldError
  puts ex.message
end
```

For proto2-style required fields, generated code keeps the value surface non-nilable where practical
and exposes presence separately:

```crystal
settings = Example::Settings.new
settings.retries = 3
settings.has_retries? # => true
settings.clear_retries
settings.has_retries? # => false
```

## Examples

Minimal self-contained example:

```sh
crystal run examples/basic_roundtrip.cr
```

Example output:

```text
encoded bytes: 9
decoded id: 42
decoded name: Alice
```

Strict vs partial required-field example:

```sh
crystal run examples/required_strict_partial.cr
```

Example output:

```text
strict encode error: Missing required field: value
partial bytes: 2
strict decode error: Missing required field: value
partial child present?: true
partial child has_value?: false
```

Example for service metadata / extension metadata generation:

```sh
crystal run examples/service_extensions_codegen.cr
```

This example constructs descriptors inline and shows that the generated code includes output like the following.

```crystal
module DemoService
  METHODS = [
    {
      name: "Ping",
      request_type: ".demo.PingRequest",
      response_type: ".demo.PingReply",
      client_streaming: false,
      server_streaming: false,
      path: "/demo.DemoService/Ping",
    },
  ] of NamedTuple(name: String, request_type: String, response_type: String, client_streaming: Bool, server_streaming: Bool, path: String)
end

module Extensions
  RPC_REQUIRED = ExtensionDescriptor.new(
    name: "rpc_required",
    extendee: ".google.protobuf.MethodOptions",
    number: 50001,
    label: Proto::Bootstrap::FieldLabel::LABEL_OPTIONAL,
    type: Proto::Bootstrap::FieldType::TYPE_BOOL,
    type_name: "",
  )
end
```

## Testing

```sh
crystal spec
```

def.name: decode
def.body:
msg = new
reader = Wire::Reader.new(io)
while tag = reader.read_tag
  fn, wt = tag
  case fn
  when 1
    msg.file_to_generate << reader.read_string
  when 2
    msg.parameter = reader.read_string
  when 15
    msg.proto_file << (FileDescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  else
    msg.capture_unknown_field(reader, fn, wt)
  end
end
msg

def.name: encode
def.body:
w = Wire::Writer.new(io)
file_to_generate.each do |file_name| w.write_string_field(1, file_name) end
w.write_string_field(2, parameter)
proto_file.each do |file_desc| w.write_embedded(15) do |sub| file_desc.encode(sub) end end
write_unknown_fields(w)

def.name: decode
def.body:
msg = new
reader = Wire::Reader.new(io)
while tag = reader.read_tag
  fn, wt = tag
  case fn
  when 1
    msg.name = reader.read_string
  when 2
    msg.field << (FieldDescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  when 6
    msg.extension << (FieldDescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  when 3
    msg.nested_type << (DescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  when 4
    msg.enum_type << (EnumDescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  when 7
    msg.options = MessageOptions.decode(reader.read_embedded)
  when 8
    msg.oneof_decl << (OneofDescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  else
    msg.capture_unknown_field(reader, fn, wt)
  end
end
msg

def.name: encode
def.body:
w = Wire::Writer.new(io)
w.write_string_field(1, name)
field.each do |field_desc| w.write_embedded(2) do |sub| field_desc.encode(sub) end end
extension.each do |extension_desc| w.write_embedded(6) do |sub| extension_desc.encode(sub) end end
nested_type.each do |nested_msg| w.write_embedded(3) do |sub| nested_msg.encode(sub) end end
enum_type.each do |enum_desc| w.write_embedded(4) do |sub| enum_desc.encode(sub) end end
if opts = options
  w.write_embedded(7) do |sub| opts.encode(sub) end
end
oneof_decl.each do |oneof_desc| w.write_embedded(8) do |sub| oneof_desc.encode(sub) end end
write_unknown_fields(w)

def.name: decode
def.body:
msg = new
reader = Wire::Reader.new(io)
while tag = reader.read_tag
  fn, wt = tag
  case fn
  when 1
    msg.name = reader.read_string
  when 2
    msg.extendee = reader.read_string
  when 3
    msg.number = reader.read_int32
  when 4
    msg.label = FieldLabel.from_value(reader.read_int32)
  when 5
    msg.type = FieldType.from_value(reader.read_int32)
  when 6
    msg.type_name = reader.read_string
  when 7
    msg.default_value = reader.read_string
  when 8
    msg.options = FieldOptions.decode(reader.read_embedded)
  when 9
    msg.oneof_index = reader.read_int32
  when 10
    msg.json_name = reader.read_string
  when 17
    msg.proto3_optional = reader.read_bool
  else
    msg.capture_unknown_field(reader, fn, wt)
  end
end
msg

def.name: encode
def.body:
w = Wire::Writer.new(io)
w.write_string_field(1, name)
w.write_string_field(2, extendee)
w.write_int32_field(3, number)
w.write_tag(4, WireType::VARINT)
w.write_int32(label.value)
w.write_tag(5, WireType::VARINT)
w.write_int32(type.value)
w.write_string_field(6, type_name)
w.write_string_field(7, default_value)
if opts = options
  w.write_embedded(8) do |sub| opts.encode(sub) end
end
if oi = oneof_index
  w.write_tag(9, WireType::VARINT)
  w.write_int32(oi)
end
w.write_string_field(10, json_name)
w.write_bool_field(17, proto3_optional?)
write_unknown_fields(w)

def.name: decode
def.body:
msg = new
reader = Wire::Reader.new(io)
while tag = reader.read_tag
  fn, wt = tag
  case fn
  when 1
    msg.name = reader.read_string
  when 2
    msg.package = reader.read_string
  when 3
    msg.dependency << reader.read_string
  when 4
    msg.message_type << (DescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  when 5
    msg.enum_type << (EnumDescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  when 7
    msg.extension << (FieldDescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  when 6
    msg.service << (ServiceDescriptorProto.decode(reader.read_embedded))
  when 12
    msg.syntax = reader.read_string
  else
    msg.capture_unknown_field(reader, fn, wt)
  end
end
msg

def.name: encode
def.body:
w = Wire::Writer.new(io)
w.write_string_field(1, name)
w.write_string_field(2, package)
dependency.each do |dependency_name| w.write_string_field(3, dependency_name) end
message_type.each do |message_desc| w.write_embedded(4) do |sub| message_desc.encode(sub) end end
enum_type.each do |enum_desc| w.write_embedded(5) do |sub| enum_desc.encode(sub) end end
extension.each do |extension_desc| w.write_embedded(7) do |sub| extension_desc.encode(sub) end end
service.each do |svc| w.write_embedded(6) do |sub| svc.encode(sub) end end
w.write_string_field(12, syntax)
write_unknown_fields(w)

end

  when 1
    msg.name = reader.read_string
  when 2
    msg.input_type = reader.read_string
  when 3
    msg.output_type = reader.read_string
  when 4

  when 5

  when 6

end

end

w.write_bool_field(6, server_streaming?)

def.name: resolve
def.body:
resolved = if index = @index
  if entry = index.resolve(proto_type)
    entry.crystal_name
  else
    NamingPolicy.fq_type_to_crystal(proto_type)
  end
else
  NamingPolicy.fq_type_to_crystal(proto_type)
end
if current_package.empty?
  return resolved
end

package_module = NamingPolicy.package_to_module(current_package)
if package_module
else
  return resolved
end

prefix = "#{package_module}::"
if resolved.starts_with?(prefix)
else
  return resolved
end

resolved[prefix.size, resolved.bytesize - prefix.size]

def.body:

emit_header(io)
emit_requires(io)
emit_body(io)
io.to_s

def.name: crystal_keyword?
def.body:
case name
when "abstract", "alias", "annotation", "as", "asm", "begin", "break", "case", "class", "def", "do", "else", "elsif", "end", "ensure", "enum", "extend", "for", "fun", "if", "in", "include", "instance_sizeof", "is_a?", "lib", "macro", "module", "next", "nil", "nil?", "of", "out", "pointerof", "private", "protected", "require", "rescue", "responds_to?", "return", "select", "self", "sizeof", "struct", "super", "then", "type", "typeof", "uninitialized", "union", "unless", "until", "verbatim", "when", "while", "with", "yield"
  true
else
  false
end

def.name: enum_member_name
def.body:
normalized = name.gsub(/[^A-Za-z0-9_]/, "_")
if normalized.empty?
  normalized = "VALUE"
elsif normalized[0].ascii_number?
  normalized = "VALUE_#{normalized}"
end

if normalized[0].ascii_lowercase?
  normalized = normalized.upcase
end

normalized

types.name: NamingPolicy
types.doc:
NamingPolicy converts proto identifiers to Crystal identifiers.

Rules (matching protoc-gen-go / protoc-gen-python conventions adapted for Crystal):
  - package "foo.bar_baz" → module Foo::BarBaz
  - message "HelloRequest" → class HelloRequest  (already CamelCase)
  - enum "Status" → enum Status
  - enum value "STATUS_OK" → STATUS_OK  (kept as-is, Crystal enums use SCREAMING_SNAKE)
  - field "my_field" → property my_field  (snake_case, kept)
types.summary: NamingPolicy converts proto identifiers to Crystal identifiers.

def.name: fq_type_to_crystal
def.body:
s = fq.lstrip('.')
parts = s.split('.')




pkg_parts = [] of String
type_parts = [] of String
parts.each do |part_name|
  if type_parts.empty? && part_name[0]?.try(&.lowercase?)
    pkg_parts << (camelize(part_name))
  else
    type_parts << part_name
  end
end
(pkg_parts + type_parts).join("::")

Convert a proto message/enum name (already CamelCase) to a Crystal class/enum name.
Nested names are NOT joined here — the caller passes the leaf name.

"" → nil

def.name: package_to_module
def.body:
if package.empty?
  return
end
(package.split('.')).map do |part| camelize(part) end.join("::")

TypeIndex builds a flat index of all types defined in the proto files
being compiled, keyed by fully-qualified proto name (e.g. ".foo.bar.HelloRequest").
Used by the code generator to resolve field type_names.
types.summary: TypeIndex builds a flat index of all types defined in the proto files being compiled, keyed by fully-qualified proto name (e.g.

def.name: resolve
def.body:
if mapped = @map[proto_type]?
  return mapped
end

raise(ArgumentError.new("Missing type_map entry for #{proto_type}"))

def.name: build
def.body:
index = TypeIndex.new(files)
map = parse_type_map(parameter)
if map.empty?
  return CanonicalTypeNameResolver.new(index)
end

TypeMapNameResolver.new(map)

types.name: HasUnknownFields
types.doc:
Mixin that adds unknown field capture and re-serialization to a message class.

Include this module inside any class that inherits Proto::Message to get:
  capture_unknown_field(reader, field_number, wire_type)
  write_unknown_fields(writer)
types.summary: Mixin that adds unknown field capture and re-serialization to a message class.

instance_methods.name: add_unknown_varint
instance_methods.doc:
Append a raw unknown varint value when a field cannot be decoded semantically
(for example, unknown enum numeric values under strict enum APIs).
instance_methods.summary: Append a raw unknown varint value when a field cannot be decoded semantically (for example, unknown enum numeric values under strict enum APIs).

instance_methods.name: capture_unknown_field
instance_methods.args_string: (reader : Wire::Reader, field_number : Int32, wire_type : Int32) : Nil

def.name: decode_known_enum
def.body:
begin
  raw = Wire::Reader.int32_from_varint(raw_u64)
  yield raw
rescue ArgumentError
  add_unknown_varint(field_number, raw_u64)
end

instance_methods.name: extension_value?
instance_methods.doc: Returns the last matching extension value (proto singular semantics) or nil.
instance_methods.summary: Returns the last matching extension value (proto singular semantics) or nil.

instance_methods.name: extension_values
instance_methods.doc: Returns all matching extension values in wire order.
instance_methods.summary: Returns all matching extension values in wire order.

def.name: extension_values
def.body:
expected_wire_type = extension_wire_type_for(descriptor.type)
if expected_wire_type
else
  return [] of ExtensionValue
end

values = [] of ExtensionValue
unknown_fields.each do |unknown_field|
  if unknown_field.field_number == descriptor.number
  else
    next
  end
  if unknown_field.wire_type == expected_wire_type
  else
    next
  end

  value = decode_extension_value(unknown_field, descriptor.type)
  if value.nil?
  else
    values << value
  end
end

values

instance_methods.name: has_extension?
instance_methods.doc:
Returns true when at least one unknown field matches the descriptor and can
be decoded as an extension value.
instance_methods.summary: Returns true when at least one unknown field matches the descriptor and can be decoded as an extension value.

def.name: write_unknown_fields
def.body:
@unknown_fields.each do |unknown_field|
  writer.write_tag(unknown_field.field_number, unknown_field.wire_type)
  case d = unknown_field.data
  when UInt64
    if unknown_field.wire_type == WireType::FIXED64
      writer.write_fixed64(d)
    else
      writer.write_varint(d)
    end
  when UInt32
    writer.write_fixed32(d)
  when Bytes
    writer.write_bytes(d)
  when Array(UnknownField)
    write_unknown_group_fields(writer, d)
    writer.write_tag(unknown_field.field_number, WireType::END_GROUP)
  end
end

types.name: Message
types.doc:
Base module for generated message classes.

Concrete generated classes include this module and implement:
  def encode(io : IO) : Nil
  def self.decode(io : IO) : self

The Bytes convenience methods are provided here.

instance_methods.doc: Serialize this message to a byte slice.
instance_methods.summary: Serialize this message to a byte slice.

instance_methods.doc: See `Object#hash(hasher)`

instance_methods.name: inspect
instance_methods.doc:
Appends this struct's name and instance variables names and values
to the given IO.

```
struct Point
  def initialize(@x : Int32, @y : Int32)
  end
end

p1 = Point.new 1, 2
p1.to_s    # "Point(@x=1, @y=2)"
p1.inspect # "Point(@x=1, @y=2)"
```
instance_methods.summary: Appends this struct's name and instance variables names and values to the given IO.

def.name: inspect
def.body:
io << "Proto::OpenEnum("
io << ({{ T.resolve.stringify }})
io << ").new("
if known_value = known
  io << known_value
else
  io << raw
end
io << ')'

instance_methods.name: to_s
instance_methods.doc: Same as `#inspect(io)`.

def.name: to_s
def.body:
if known_value = known
  known_value.to_s(io)
else
  raw.to_s(io)
end

types.name: UnknownField
types.doc:
Holds one unknown field captured during message decoding.
Preserves wire_type so the field can be faithfully re-encoded.
types.summary: Holds one unknown field captured during message decoding.

types.name: Reader
types.doc:
Reader wraps an IO and provides typed protobuf wire-format read primitives.

Entry point for message decode loops is `read_tag`, which returns nil on
clean EOF (end of message) and raises DecodeError on malformed input.
All other read methods raise DecodeError on unexpected EOF.
types.summary: Reader wraps an IO and provides typed protobuf wire-format read primitives.

def.name: int32_from_varint
def.body:
begin
  upper = raw >> 32
  if (upper == 0_u64) || (upper == 4294967295_u64)
    raw.to_u32!.unsafe_as(Int32)
  else
    raise(DecodeError.new("int32 out of range: #{raw}"))
  end
rescue OverflowError
  raise(DecodeError.new("int32 out of range: #{raw}"))
end

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_string: (io : IO, max_message_size : Int64 = DEFAULT_MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE, max_field_length : Int32 = DEFAULT_MAX_FIELD_LENGTH)

def.name: expect_wire_type!
def.body:
if (wire_type == expected_wire_type) || (wire_type == alternate_wire_type)
  return
end
expected = "#{WireType.name(expected_wire_type)} or #{WireType.name(alternate_wire_type)}"
raise_wire_type_mismatch(field_number, wire_type, expected)

instance_methods.name: packed_wire_type?
instance_methods.args_string: (field_number : Int32, wire_type : Int32, unpacked_wire_type : Int32) : Bool
instance_methods.args_html: (field_number : Int32, wire_type : Int32, unpacked_wire_type : Int32) : Bool

def.name: packed_wire_type?
def.body:
if wire_type == WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED
  return true
end
if wire_type == unpacked_wire_type
  return false
end
expect_wire_type!(field_number, wire_type, WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED, unpacked_wire_type)
false

def.name: read_bytes
def.body:
begin
  len = read_length
  slice = Bytes.new(len)
  @io.read_fully(slice)
  consume_bytes!(len.to_i64)
  slice
rescue IO::EOFError
  raise(DecodeError.new("unexpected EOF"))
end

def.name: read_double
def.body:
begin
  value = @io.read_bytes(Float64, IO::ByteFormat::LittleEndian)
  consume_bytes!(8)
  value
rescue IO::EOFError
  raise(DecodeError.new("unexpected EOF"))
end

instance_methods.name: read_embedded
instance_methods.doc:
Return a scoped IO::Memory over the next length-delimited field.
The bytes are consumed from this reader immediately.
instance_methods.summary: Return a scoped IO::Memory over the next length-delimited field.

def.body:
begin
  value = @io.read_bytes(UInt32, IO::ByteFormat::LittleEndian)
  consume_bytes!(4)
  value
rescue IO::EOFError
  raise(DecodeError.new("unexpected EOF"))

instance_methods.doc: int32 / int64 use sign-extended varint

instance_methods.name: read_packed_double
instance_methods.doc: Decode a packed repeated double field.

def.name: read_packed_double

read_packed_fixed_values(8) do |sub_io|
  yield sub_io.read_bytes(Float64, IO::ByteFormat::LittleEndian)
end

instance_methods.doc: Decode a packed repeated fixed32 field.

  yield sub_io.read_bytes(UInt32, IO::ByteFormat::LittleEndian)

instance_methods.doc: Decode a packed repeated float field.

  yield sub_io.read_bytes(Int64, IO::ByteFormat::LittleEndian)

instance_methods.name: read_packed_varint
instance_methods.doc:
Decode a packed repeated varint field into the given array.
Yields each decoded UInt64 raw value; callers convert as needed.
instance_methods.summary: Decode a packed repeated varint field into the given array.

def.name: read_packed_varint
def.body:
sub_io = read_embedded
sub = Reader.new(sub_io)
while sub_io.pos < sub_io.size
  yield sub.read_uint64
end

def.name: read_sfixed32
def.body:
begin
  value = @io.read_bytes(Int32, IO::ByteFormat::LittleEndian)
  consume_bytes!(4)
  value
rescue IO::EOFError
  raise(DecodeError.new("unexpected EOF"))
end

def.name: read_sfixed64
def.body:
begin
  value = @io.read_bytes(Int64, IO::ByteFormat::LittleEndian)
  consume_bytes!(8)
  value
rescue IO::EOFError
  raise(DecodeError.new("unexpected EOF"))
end

instance_methods.name: read_sint32
instance_methods.doc: sint32 / sint64 use zigzag encoding

instance_methods.name: read_tag
instance_methods.doc:
Read the next field tag.
Returns {field_number, wire_type}, or nil on clean EOF.

def.name: read_tag
def.body:
begin
  v = read_varint? do return end
  wire_type = (v & 7_u64).to_i32
  field_number = (v >> 3).to_i32!
  if field_number <= 0
    raise(DecodeError.new("invalid field number: #{field_number}"))
  end
  if field_number > MAX_FIELD_NUMBER
    raise(DecodeError.new("field number out of range: #{field_number}"))
  end
  if WireType.valid?(wire_type)
  else
    raise(DecodeError.new("invalid wire type: #{wire_type}"))
  end
  {field_number, wire_type}
rescue OverflowError
  raise(DecodeError.new("field number out of range"))
end

instance_methods.name: read_uint32
instance_methods.doc: uint32 / uint64 use plain varint

def.name: read_unknown_field
def.body:
case wire_type
when WireType::VARINT
  UnknownField.new(field_number, WireType::VARINT, read_uint64)
when WireType::FIXED64
  UnknownField.new(field_number, WireType::FIXED64, read_fixed64)
when WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED
  UnknownField.new(field_number, WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED, read_bytes)
when WireType::FIXED32
  UnknownField.new(field_number, WireType::FIXED32, read_fixed32)
when WireType::START_GROUP
  UnknownField.new(field_number, WireType::START_GROUP, read_unknown_group(field_number))
when WireType::END_GROUP
  raise(DecodeError.new("unexpected END_GROUP"))
else
  skip_tag({field_number, wire_type})
  raise(DecodeError.new("unknown wire type: #{wire_type}"))
end

instance_methods.name: skip_field
instance_methods.doc: Skip one untagged field value of the given wire type.
instance_methods.summary: Skip one untagged field value of the given wire type.

instance_methods.name: skip_tag
instance_methods.doc: Skip one tagged field value with strict START_GROUP/END_GROUP matching.
instance_methods.summary: Skip one tagged field value with strict START_GROUP/END_GROUP matching.

types.name: Writer
types.doc:
Writer wraps an IO and provides typed protobuf wire-format write primitives.

Typical message encode pattern:
  writer.write_tag(1, WireType::VARINT)
  writer.write_int32(value)

For embedded messages use write_embedded which buffers the sub-message,
then writes the length prefix followed by the bytes.
types.summary: Writer wraps an IO and provides typed protobuf wire-format write primitives.

instance_methods.name: write_bool_field
instance_methods.doc: Write a bool field only if value is true.
instance_methods.summary: Write a bool field only if value is true.

def.name: write_bool_field
def.body:
if value
else
  return
end
write_tag(field_number, WireType::VARINT)
write_bool(value)

instance_methods.name: write_bytes_field
instance_methods.doc: Write a bytes field only if value is not empty.
instance_methods.summary: Write a bytes field only if value is not empty.

def.name: write_bytes_field
def.body:
if value.empty?
  return
end
write_tag(field_number, WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED)
write_bytes(value)

instance_methods.name: write_embedded
instance_methods.doc:
Write a length-delimited field by buffering the block's output.
Yields the underlying IO::Memory; the accumulated bytes are length-prefixed and written.
instance_methods.summary: Write a length-delimited field by buffering the block's output.

def.name: write_embedded
def.body:
buf = IO::Memory.new
yield buf
validate_field_length!(buf.size)
data = buf.to_slice
write_tag(field_number, WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED)
write_bytes(data)

instance_methods.name: write_int32
instance_methods.doc: int32: sign-extends to 64 bits (negative values become 10-byte varints)
instance_methods.summary: int32: sign-extends to 64 bits (negative values become 10-byte varints)

instance_methods.name: write_int32_field
instance_methods.doc: Write an int32 field only if value != 0.
instance_methods.summary: Write an int32 field only if value != 0.

def.name: write_int32_field
def.body:
if value == 0
  return
end
write_tag(field_number, WireType::VARINT)
write_int32(value)

instance_methods.name: write_packed
instance_methods.doc:
Write a packed repeated field using the block to write each element into the buffer.
Skips writing the field entirely if the array is empty.
instance_methods.summary: Write a packed repeated field using the block to write each element into the buffer.

def.name: write_packed
def.body:
buf = IO::Memory.new
yield buf
if buf.size == 0
  return
end
validate_field_length!(buf.size)
write_tag(field_number, WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED)
write_bytes(buf.to_slice)

instance_methods.name: write_sint32
instance_methods.doc: sint32 / sint64 use zigzag encoding

instance_methods.name: write_string_field
instance_methods.doc: Write a string field only if value is not empty.
instance_methods.summary: Write a string field only if value is not empty.

def.name: write_string_field
def.body:
if value.empty?
  return
end
write_tag(field_number, WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED)
write_string(value)

instance_methods.name: write_uint64_field
instance_methods.doc: Write a uint64 field only if value != 0.
instance_methods.summary: Write a uint64 field only if value != 0.

def.name: write_uint64_field
def.body:
if value == 0_u64
  return
end
write_tag(field_number, WireType::VARINT)
write_uint64(value)

def.name: write_varint
def.body:
loop do
  byte = (value & 127).to_u8
  value = value >> 7
  if value == 0
    @io.write_byte(byte)
    break
  else
    @io.write_byte(byte | 128_u8)
  end
end

def.name: name
def.body:
case wire_type
when VARINT
  "Proto::WireType::VARINT"
when FIXED64
  "Proto::WireType::FIXED64"
when LENGTH_DELIMITED
  "Proto::WireType::LENGTH_DELIMITED"
when START_GROUP
  "Proto::WireType::START_GROUP"
when END_GROUP
  "Proto::WireType::END_GROUP"
when FIXED32
  "Proto::WireType::FIXED32"
else
  wire_type.to_s
end
